message:
chore(node): update beads issue tracker state

Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with current issue status — reflects newly
created and updated beads from recent development activity including
migration scanner optimization and monorepo throughput work items.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2q5","title":"[10.6] Optimize migration scanner throughput for large monorepos.","description":"## [10.6] Optimize Migration Scanner Throughput for Large Monorepos

### Why This Exists

Section 9D.2 mandates "optimize scan and transform throughput with deterministic batching and cache reuse." The migration scanner (10.3) must handle monorepos with 10,000+ source files without unacceptable wall-clock time. Current scanning is sequential and re-scans unchanged files on every run. For enterprise adoption, migration operations must complete within minutes, not hours, even on the largest codebases. This bead makes the scanner production-ready for monorepo-scale workloads.

### What It Must Do

Implement three optimization strategies for the migration scanner:

**Incremental scanning**: Maintain a scan cache that records file path, content hash, and last scan result. On subsequent runs, only re-scan files whose content hash has changed. Cache invalidation must be deterministic — given the same file set and content, the cache always produces the same result regardless of run order or timing. The cache format is versioned so upgrades don't silently use stale data.

**Parallel file processing**: Partition the file set into batches and process batches in parallel using a configurable worker pool. Batch assignment must be deterministic (sorted by path, then chunked) so results are reproducible. The worker count defaults to available CPU cores but is overridable via `--workers N`.

**Cache reuse across runs**: The scan cache persists to disk between runs (default location: `.franken_node/scan_cache.json`). Cache entries expire after a configurable TTL (default: 7 days) to prevent unbounded growth. A `--clear-cache` flag forces a full rescan.

Before/after benchmarks must be collected on representative monorepo fixtures (synthetic fixture with 10k+ files of varying sizes and complexity). Benchmarks measure wall-clock time, files-per-second throughput, cache hit ratio, and peak memory.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. Incremental scanning skips files whose content hash matches the cached hash; a 10k-file re-scan with 1% changes completes in under 10% of the full-scan time.
2. Parallel processing with N workers achieves near-linear speedup up to 4 workers on the reference fixture.
3. Deterministic batching: two runs with identical input produce byte-identical scan results regardless of worker count.
4. Scan cache persists to `.franken_node/scan_cache.json` with a versioned format; format version mismatch triggers full rescan with a warning.
5. Cache entries expire after configurable TTL; `--clear-cache` forces full rescan.
6. Before/after benchmarks recorded in `artifacts/section_10_6/bd-2q5/benchmark_comparison.json`.
7. Verification script `scripts/check_scanner_throughput.py` with `--json` flag validates throughput targets.
8. Unit tests in `tests/test_check_scanner_throughput.py` cover cache logic, batch partitioning, hash computation, and TTL expiration.

### Key Dependencies

- Migration scanner from 10.3.
- Synthetic monorepo fixture generator (or checked-in fixture).
- Content hashing library (SHA-256 or BLAKE3).

### Testing & Logging Requirements

- Determinism test: run twice with same input and different worker counts, assert identical results.
- Cache hit test: run, modify one file, re-run, assert only modified file is re-scanned.
- TTL test: create cache entry with expired TTL, run, assert re-scan occurs.
- Structured JSON logs for each run: total files, cache hits, cache misses, workers used, wall-clock time, files/second.

### Expected Artifacts

- Optimized scanner code in `crates/franken-node/src/` or `scripts/`.
- `scripts/check_scanner_throughput.py` — verification script.
- `tests/test_check_scanner_throughput.py` — unit tests.
- `artifacts/section_10_6/bd-2q5/benchmark_comparison.json` — before/after benchmarks.
- `artifacts/section_10_6/bd-2q5/verification_evidence.json` — gate results.
- `artifacts/section_10_6/bd-2q5/verification_summary.md` — human-readable summary.

### Additional E2E Requirement

- E2E test scripts must execute full end-to-end workflows from clean fixtures, emit deterministic machine-readable pass/fail evidence, and capture stepwise structured logs for root-cause triage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Migration scanner throughput on a 10k-file monorepo benchmark completes within a defined time ceiling (documented in spec).
2. Scanner supports incremental mode: re-scanning after a single-file change processes only the delta, not the full tree.
3. Before/after throughput comparison table is produced per Section 7 performance doctrine.
4. Memory usage stays bounded (no O(n^2) growth) — verified with a memory profile artifact for the large-monorepo benchmark.
5. Parallelism: scanner utilizes available CPU cores (configurable concurrency level) for file traversal and analysis.
6. Correctness proof: scanner output on the golden corpus is identical before and after optimization.
7. Benchmark is reproducible from a clean checkout with a single command and produces structured JSON results.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:46.936795081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:22:15.418631953Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:22:15.418583352Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-6","self-contained","test-obligations"]}
 {"id":"bd-2q8ha","title":"Unblock package clippy by removing needless borrow in transport_fault_gate hash","description":"Why this exists: package-wide `cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -- -D warnings` is currently failing on crates/franken-node/src/connector/transport_fault_gate.rs:631 because `Sha256::update` receives an unnecessary borrowed array (`&[u8::from(passed)]`). This is unrelated to the content-hash beads but it blocks authoritative clippy validation for them. What this task must do: remove the needless borrow cleanly, preserve transport fault gate hash semantics, and rerun remote rch clippy/check to see whether the validation gate moves forward or exposes the next blocker. Acceptance criteria: 1. The transport_fault_gate lint is fixed without changing behavior. 2. Heavy cargo validation uses rch only. 3. Results are reported in Agent Mail so other bead owners know whether package clippy is still blocked elsewhere.","notes":"Patch landed in crates/franken-node/src/connector/transport_fault_gate.rs: removed the needless borrow in Sha256::update for the passed flag while preserving hash semantics. Local narrow hygiene passed: git diff --check clean; rustfmt --edition 2024 --check clean. Remote validation via rch: cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets passed cleanly on worker jain after the patch, confirming the file compiles in-package. Package cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -- -D warnings no longer stops at transport_fault_gate.rs:631, but the remote run on vmi1264463 has not yet produced a trustworthy final verdict; it remains in execute/progress-stale territory, so the next package-wide blocker is not yet authoritatively surfaced.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"AzureCanyon","created_at":"2026-03-17T00:41:53.250102930Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T06:55:28.169120662Z","closed_at":"2026-03-17T06:55:28.169093812Z","close_reason":"Transport fault gate lint fixed; later rch package clippy passed on a healthy worker","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","transport-fault-gate","validation"]}

If gates are clean and no actionable work exists, close this bead with coordination evidence and no-action justification.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MaroonCastle","created_at":"2026-04-01T00:45:23.614528303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-01T00:51:40.096819110Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T00:51:40.096795115Z","close_reason":"No actionable open backlog; tracked source tree unchanged; recent remote cargo check/clippy passes remain representative; two current rch cargo-check attempts stalled at worker level after normal startup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2qdye","title":"Make engine binary path fully configurable via Config struct and CLI flag (ops/engine_dispatcher.rs)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:41.083669293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:37:09.230351465Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:37:09.230328422Z","close_reason":"Implemented [engine].binary_path config + FRANKEN_NODE_ENGINE_BINARY_PATH override, added --engine-bin PathBuf flag, and wired run precedence as CLI > FRANKEN_ENGINE_BIN > config; rch cargo verification blocked externally by /data/projects/asupersync/src/channel/deadlock_test.rs missing and /data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/bifurcation_boundary_scanner.rs:567 missing distance_to_threshold_millionths","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":352,"issue_id":"bd-2qdye","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GOAL: Make the franken_engine binary path fully configurable through all three layers: Config struct, CLI flag, and environment variable.

CURRENT STATE (ops/engine_dispatcher.rs):
  - FRANKEN_ENGINE_BIN env var: EXISTS and has highest priority (line 96)
  - Config file option: MISSING (no engine_bin_path field in Config)
  - CLI flag: MISSING (no --engine-bin in RunArgs)
  - main.rs uses EngineDispatcher::default() which ignores config entirely (line 3984)
  - Hard-coded absolute paths: /data/projects/franken_engine/... and /dp/franken_engine/... (lines 52-57)

REQUIRED CHANGES:

1. Add to Config struct (config.rs):
   [engine]
   binary_path = "/path/to/franken-engine"  # optional, overrides search
   
   Add FRANKEN_NODE_ENGINE_BINARY_PATH env var override

2. Add to CLI (cli.rs):
   RunArgs { ... engine_bin: Option<PathBuf> ... }
   with --engine-bin flag

3. Update main.rs to pass config/CLI to EngineDispatcher:
   let engine_path = args.engine_bin
       .or_else(|| config.engine.binary_path.clone())
       .or_else(|| std::env::var("FRANKEN_ENGINE_BIN").ok().map(PathBuf::from));
   let dispatcher = EngineDispatcher::new(engine_path);

4. Update EngineDispatcher to accept optional override in constructor

PRECEDENCE (highest to lowest):
  1. CLI: --engine-bin /path
  2. Env: FRANKEN_ENGINE_BIN=/path
  3. Config: [engine] binary_path = "/path"
  4. Search candidates (relative to workspace, then sibling dirs)

BLOCKS: bd-bkpz1 (removing hard-coded absolute paths — do that after config path works)
","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qf","title":"[10.2] Implement compatibility behavior registry with typed shim metadata.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.2 — Compatibility Core

Why This Exists:
Compatibility core as strategic wedge: policy-visible behavior, divergence receipts, L1/L2 lockstep, and spec-first fixture governance.

Task Objective:
Implement compatibility behavior registry with typed shim metadata.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_2/bd-2qf_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-2qf/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_2/bd-2qf/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.2] Implement compatibility behavior registry with typed shim metadata.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.2] Implement compatibility behavior registry with typed shim metadata." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.2] Implement compatibility behavior registry with typed shim metadata." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.2] Implement compatibility behavior registry with typed shim metadata." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.2] Implement compatibility behavior registry with typed shim metadata." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:43.914447842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T09:34:03.425939378Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T09:34:03.425914892Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-2","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2qf","depends_on_id":"bd-2wz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qin","title":"NaN/Inf fail-open in bpet_migration_gate: evaluate_admission and evaluate_rollout_health use >= comparisons on f64 without is_finite() guards, causing NaN values to silently pass as Allow/no-rollback","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-03-07T12:35:21.783887886Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-07T12:35:38.543778359Z","closed_at":"2026-03-07T12:35:38.543668625Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-3epz","title":"[10.14] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.14

Task Objective:
Create a hard section completion gate that verifies all implemented behavior in this section with comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e scripts, and detailed structured logging evidence.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Section test matrix covers happy-path, edge-case, and adversarial/error-path scenarios for all section deliverables.
- E2E scripts execute representative end-user workflows and policy/control-plane transitions for this section.
- Structured logs include stable event/error codes, trace correlation IDs, and enough context for deterministic replay triage.
- Verification report is deterministic and machine-readable for CI/release gating.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-level test matrix and coverage summary artifact.
- E2E script suite with pass/fail outputs and reproducible fixtures/seeds.
- Structured log validation report and traceability bundle.
- Gate verdict artifact consumable by release automation.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-3epz/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-3epz/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests must validate contracts, invariants, and failure semantics.
- E2E tests must validate cross-component behavior from user/operator entrypoints to persisted effects.
- Logging must support root-cause analysis without hidden context requirements.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.14] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.14] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.14] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.14] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.14] Section-wide verification gate: comprehensive unit+e2e+logging" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"Section test matrix covers happy-path, edge-case, and adversarial/error-path scenarios for all section deliverables.; E2E scripts execute representative end-user workflows and policy/control-plane transitions for this section.; Structured logs include stable event/error codes, trace correlation IDs, and enough context for deterministic replay triage.; Verification report is deterministic and machine-readable for CI/release gating.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:48:11.517555268Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:17:00.676536385Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:17:00.676497252Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","test-obligations","verification-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-126h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-129f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-12n3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-15u3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-18ud","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-1ayu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-1dar","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-1daz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-1dpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-1fck","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3epz","depends_on_id":"bd-1fp4"  {"id":"bd-3ex","title":"[10.7] Add verifier CLI conformance contract tests.","description":"## [10.7] Verifier CLI Conformance Contract Tests

### Why This Exists

The verifier CLI (`franken-node verify`) is a public interface used by external verifiers, CI pipelines, and the verifier economy (10.17, 10.12). Its behavior must be stable and predictable across versions — breaking changes to input formats, output schemas, exit codes, or error messages would break downstream consumers silently. This bead establishes contract tests that pin the CLI's observable behavior, detect unintended breaking changes, and require explicit version bumps when intentional breaking changes are made.

### What It Must Do

**Contract definition**: Define the verifier CLI's contract in a machine-readable format (`spec/verifier_cli_contract.toml` or similar). The contract specifies: (1) accepted input formats (file paths, stdin, glob patterns), (2) output JSON schema for success and error cases, (3) exit codes and their meanings (0 = all checks pass, 1 = some checks fail, 2 = invalid input, 3 = internal error), (4) error message format (structured JSON with error code, human message, and remediation hint), and (5) command-line flag inventory with types and defaults.

**Contract test suite**: A test suite that exercises every aspect of the contract with specific, pinned inputs and expected outputs. Tests use snapshot-style assertions — the expected output is stored alongside the test and compared byte-for-byte (after normalization of timestamps and paths). Adding a new flag or output field is non-breaking; removing or changing one is breaking.

**Breaking change detection**: When a contract test fails, the failure message clearly distinguishes between "new field added" (non-breaking, auto-update snapshot) and "existing field changed/removed" (breaking, requires version bump). A `--update-snapshots` flag regenerates expected outputs for non-breaking changes.

**Version enforcement**: The CLI embeds a contract version (semver). Breaking changes increment the major version. The contract test suite validates that the embedded version matches the contract definition's version. If a breaking change is detected without a version bump, the gate fails.

**Backward compatibility layer**: When a major version bump occurs, the CLI supports `--compat-version=<N>` to produce output in the previous format for one major version back, giving consumers time to migrate.

### Acceptance Criteria

1. Verifier CLI contract is defined in a machine-readable file specifying input formats, output schemas, exit codes, error formats, and flag inventory.
2. Contract test suite covers every exit code, every output field, every error code, and every accepted input format with pinned expected outputs.
3. Breaking vs. non-breaking change detection works: new fields pass, changed/removed fields fail with clear breaking-change message.
4. Contract version is embedded in CLI output and validated against the contract definition.
5. `--update-snapshots` regenerates expected outputs for non-breaking changes only.
6. `--compat-version=<N>` produces output in the previous major version's format.
7. Verification script `scripts/check_verifier_contract.py` with `--json` flag validates the contract test suite.
8. Unit tests in `tests/test_check_verifier_contract.py` cover contract parsing, snapshot comparison, breaking change detection, version validation, and compat-version output.

### Key Dependencies

- Verifier CLI implementation (`franken-node verify` subcommand).
- CLI framework from bd-n9r (cli.rs).
- JSON Schema for output validation.
- Existing verification scripts that the CLI orchestrates.

### Testing & Logging Requirements

- Stability test: run contract tests against current CLI, assert all pass.
- Breaking change test: modify an output field, run tests, assert breaking-change failure.
- Non-breaking change test: add a new output field, run tests, assert pass (or auto-update).
- Compat-version test: request previous version output, assert format matches previous contract.
- Structured JSON logs for each contract test execution: test name, expected vs. actual, pass/fail, breaking/non-breaking classification.

### Expected Artifacts

- `spec/verifier_cli_contract.toml` — contract definition.
- `tests/contract/snapshots/` — pinned expected outputs.
- `scripts/check_verifier_contract.py` — verification script.
- `tests/test_check_verifier_contract.py` — unit tests.
- `artifacts/section_10_7/bd-3ex/verification_evidence.json` — gate results.
- `artifacts/section_10_7/bd-3ex/verification_summary.md` — human-readable summary.

### Additional E2E Requirement

- E2E test scripts must execute full end-to-end workflows from clean fixtures, emit deterministic machine-readable pass/fail evidence, and capture stepwise structured logs for root-cause triage.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Verifier CLI exposes at least: verify-module, verify-migration, verify-compatibility, and verify-corpus subcommands.
2. Each subcommand has a conformance contract defined in a spec document under docs/specs/ specifying inputs, outputs, exit codes, and error formats.
3. Contract tests exercise every specified input/output combination including edge cases and error paths.
4. Tests validate that CLI output conforms to the documented JSON schema (no undocumented fields, no missing required fields).
5. Exit codes follow a documented taxonomy: 0=pass, 1=fail, 2=error, 3=skipped.
6. Per Section 3.2 capability #10 (public verifier toolkit): CLI is usable by an external party with no internal knowledge — contract tests verify this by running in an isolated environment with no access to internal state.
7. Contract tests are generated from the spec documents (not hand-written) to ensure spec and tests stay in sync.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:47.589413741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T02:58:30.655388646Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T02:58:30.655352739Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-7","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3ex","depends_on_id":"bd-2ja","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3f9","title":"[10.3] Build deterministic migration failure replay tooling.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.3 — Migration System

Why This Exists:
Migration autopilot pipeline from audit to rollout, designed to collapse migration friction with deterministic confidence and replayability.

Task Objective:
Build deterministic migration failure replay tooling.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Implement with explicit success/failure invariants and deterministic behavior under normal, edge, and fault scenarios.
- Ensure outcomes are verifiable via automated tests and reproducible artifact outputs.

Expected Artifacts:
- Section-owned design/spec artifact at `docs/specs/section_10_3/bd-3f9_contract.md`, capturing decision rationale, invariants, and interface boundaries.
- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_3/bd-3f9/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_3/bd-3f9/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.3] Build deterministic migration failure replay tooling.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.3] Build deterministic migration failure replay tooling." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.3] Build deterministic migration failure replay tooling." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.3] Build deterministic migration failure replay tooling." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.3] Build deterministic migration failure replay tooling." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:45.346743011Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T10:21:45.798113045Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T10:21:45.798088439Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-3","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3f9","depends_on_id":"bd-hg1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-qlc6","title":"[10.14] Map remote/control tasks to lane-aware scheduler classes with priority policies.","description":"## Why This Exists

The 9J enhancement maps require that all control-plane and remote-effect workloads run under explicit scheduling discipline to prevent priority inversion, lane starvation, and uncontrolled resource consumption. Without lane-aware scheduling, a flood of low-priority remote tasks can starve critical epoch transitions or marker writes, violating runtime invariant #7 (epoch barriers must complete within bounded time). This bead establishes the foundational scheduler classification that all downstream lane-policy consumers (10.15 lane mapping, 10.11 product integration) depend on. It directly supports invariant #9 (deterministic verification gates) by making task classification auditable and telemetry-backed.

## What This Must Do

1. Define a `SchedulerLane` enum covering at minimum: `ControlCritical`, `RemoteEffect`, `Maintenance`, `Background`, each with configurable priority weight and concurrency cap.
2. Implement a `LaneMappingPolicy` that maps incoming task types (identified by `TaskClass` discriminant) to scheduler lanes based on declarative policy configuration.
3. Enforce lane starvation detection: if any lane has zero completions within a configurable time window while tasks are queued, emit a `LANE_STARVED` alert event with lane ID, queue depth, and elapsed time.
4. Enforce misclassification detection: reject tasks whose `TaskClass` does not match any policy rule and emit `LANE_MISCLASS` event with task metadata.
5. Expose lane telemetry counters: `lane_active_count`, `lane_queued_count`, `lane_completed_total`, `lane_starvation_events`, per-lane latency histograms.
6. Produce a spec document defining the lane model, priority semantics, starvation thresholds, and configuration schema.
7. Produce a conformance test that validates lane assignment correctness, starvation detection firing, and misclassification rejection under controlled load.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Task classes are mapped to lanes by policy; lane starvation and misclassification checks are enforced; lane telemetry is exposed.
- Given 4+ task classes, each maps to exactly one lane per policy; no task class maps to zero lanes.
- Starvation detection fires within 2x the configured starvation window when a lane is blocked.
- Misclassification rejection returns a typed error with the unrecognized `TaskClass` value.
- Telemetry counters increment correctly under concurrent load (verified via test harness).
- Policy changes take effect without restart (hot-reload path exists and is tested).

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: lane assignment for each task class; starvation timer triggers on blocked lane; misclassification rejection for unknown task class; policy reload applies new mapping.
- Integration tests: concurrent multi-lane load with starvation injection; lane fairness under skewed workloads; telemetry accuracy under >1000 tasks.
- Conformance tests: `tests/conformance/lane_mapping_enforcement.rs` -- normative test covering all policy rules and edge cases.
- Structured logs: `LANE_ASSIGN` (task_id, task_class, lane_id), `LANE_STARVED` (lane_id, queue_depth, elapsed_ms), `LANE_MISCLASS` (task_class, rejection_reason), `LANE_METRICS` (periodic telemetry snapshot). All events carry trace correlation IDs.

## Expected Artifacts

- `docs/specs/lane_mapping_policy.md` -- specification of lane model, priority semantics, configuration schema
- `tests/conformance/lane_mapping_enforcement.rs` -- normative conformance tests
- `artifacts/10.14/lane_mapping_metrics.csv` -- telemetry snapshot from conformance run
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-qlc6/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_14/bd-qlc6/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

- Upstream: bd-v4l0 (global remote bulkhead with `remote_max_in_flight`) -- provides the concurrency envelope this bead subdivides into lanes.
- Downstream: bd-3epz (section-wide verification gate), bd-cuut (10.15 lane mapping for control-plane workloads), bd-lus (10.11 product integration of scheduler lanes), bd-5rh (section gate).","acceptance_criteria":"Task classes are mapped to lanes by policy; lane starvation and misclassification checks are enforced; lane telemetry is exposed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:36:58.061700429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T01:44:37.220499422Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T01:44:37.220471490Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-14","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qlc6","depends_on_id":"bd-v4l0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-24T15:56:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-qlc6.1","title":"[bd-qlc6] Detect queued-lane starvation before first completion","description":"Why this exists:
`crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` only reports starvation when `last_completion_ms` is `Some(...)`. That means a lane that immediately saturates, queues additional work, and then stays blocked past the starvation window is treated as non-starved forever until the first completion happens. `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs::starvation_detected_when_lane_idle_too_long` expects the opposite behavior: queued Background work should be reported as starved after the window elapses even if the lane has never completed a task yet.

What this bug must do:
- make starvation detection use a stable baseline for lanes with queued work but no completion history yet
- keep the implementation fail-closed and deterministic
- update inline/unit/conformance coverage so the never-completed queued case is treated as starved after the configured window instead of silently ignored

Acceptance criteria:
1. `LaneScheduler::check_starvation` reports starvation for a queued lane after the configured window even if the lane has never completed a task.
2. The scheduler records accurate starvation telemetry and audit evidence for that case.
3. Inline tests in `runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` and the existing conformance expectation in `tests/conformance/control_lane_policy.rs` agree on the same behavior.
4. Validation uses `rch` for cargo-heavy checks only.","notes":"Implemented in `crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs`:\n- added `first_queued_at_ms` to track when queued work began waiting\n- starvation now uses the queued-work baseline even before any completion history exists\n- completion resets the queued baseline when work remains queued\n- inline tests now cover pre-window no-starvation, queued-before-first-completion starvation, and audit evidence\n\nVerification status:\n- `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `git diff --check -- crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs` PASS\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo check --all-targets` PASS for `frankenengine-node`; dependency warnings remain in `/data/projects/franken_engine/crates/franken-engine/src/control_plane_mock_inventory.rs`\n- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_rusticdune_bd_qlc6_1 cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` blocked by pre-existing unrelated lints in `crates/franken-node/src/connector/canonical_serializer.rs` and `crates/franken-node/src/connector/crdt.rs`\n- targeted `rch` cargo test lanes are still running/stalling in the shared worker fleet; no green test verdict captured yet

- Patched crates/franken-node/src/runtime/lane_scheduler.rs to latch starvation state, emit one LANE_STARVED event per starvation episode, and emit LANE_STARVATION_CLEARED on recovery while still returning current starvation status from check_starvation().
